run_summary: Portfolio is up +2.8% on mark-to-market across 6 open positions (including double BLDR entry). Four positions are in profit; two are slightly underwater. Track record shows strong hit rate on OVERDONE calls (64%, n=14) but weak performance on UNDERDONE (33%, n=3), and confidence-3 calls are hitting only 50%. New flag batch includes a genuine earnings shock (PLAB), a likely geopolitical fade (AVAV), a justified multiple reset (DKS), momentum extension into macro headwind (BLDR repeat), and an M&A financing signature (ONTO). Actions: HOLD all open positions as theses remain intact; add exploratory position in PLAB (earnings shock, real reset) and ONTO (M&A-financing signature worth testing); SKIP AVAV and DKS due to low conviction, and BLDR is already represented twice in portfolio.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q2 earnings release (8-K filed 2026-05-28 with items 2.02 + 9.01)
entries.thesis: An ~36% single-day collapse on 8x volume following a 2.02 earnings 8-K, in a small semi-equipment supplier, is the signature of a genuine miss + guide cut, not an overreaction. The fact that the stock was already down ~32% over five days suggests informed selling preceded the print — the magnitude of the gap is consistent with a structural reset (guide cut, customer loss, or margin collapse) rather than a one-quarter blip. Hard to call OVERDONE without seeing the actual numbers; in photomask, customer concentration risk (large foundry/IDM orders) means a single lost program can reset the multiple permanently.
entries.what_kills: If the print shows only a modest miss with no guide change and the move is driven by a single forced seller or panic — then a snapback toward the pre-drawdown level becomes likely and OVERDONE would have been the right call
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (4 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Photronics down -36.42% on -31.73% five-day move (8.38x volume) following Q2 earnings 8-K (2.02 + 9.01, filed 5/28). Confidence 4, but classified RATIONAL not OVERDONE/UNDERDONE. The flag's core thesis is sound: a 32% pre-print bleed suggests informed selling, and the -36% post-gap is more likely confirmation of a real structural reset (guide cut, margin collapse, customer loss) than a fadeable overreaction in a small, customer-concentrated semi-equipment supplier. However, the flag does NOT make a directional call—it argues the move is correctly-sized and 'hard to call OVERDONE.' This is NOT a conviction short or long, it is a 'the market may have priced this fairly, but the process and magnitude are worth studying.' Since confidence is 4 and the catalyst URL is live (exhibit-accessible 8-K), this qualifies as exploratory: a test position (6% / ~600 USD) to learn whether analyst downgrades cluster and what the actual guide says. If margin/guide cuts are real, this may be a hold or add; if the move was panic-driven and the quarter is only modestly weak, it could reverse. Exploratory tier is the right fit—high enough confidence to test, but thesis is 'study the reset,' not 'fade the panic' or 'catch the knife.'

red_team.weakest_link: The bull thesis rests on 'informed selling' preceding the gap, but five_day_change_pct of -31.73% could equally represent panic accumulation rather than orderly informed unwind.
red_team.critique: The flag argues pre-print bleeding (-31.73% over five days) proves informed selling and validates the -36% gap as structural confirmation. However, a 31.73% five-day move in a small-cap semi-equipment stock on rising volume can reflect panic, short covering cascades, or algorithmic liquidation as easily as informed unwinding. The Haiku reasoning acknowledges this is 'exploratory' and explicitly says 'If the move was panic-driven...it could reverse'—which means the bull thesis itself does not claim the move is fairly priced, only that it warrants study. Without seeing the actual 8-K exhibits (guide cut magnitude, customer loss specificity, margin compression), the claim that a -36% gap is 'consistent with structural reset' rather than 'overdone panic' is unfalsifiable on the data provided. The exploratory tier assignment is honest, but it also means the BUY is a probe, not a conviction.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Iran missile launch headlines and sector-wide defense bid (ITA +2.2%)
entries.thesis: AVAV up ~18% today on 3.3x volume with no 8-K, riding Iran missile headlines and broader defense tape (ITA +2.2%). The stock is up ~31% in five days with no company-specific catalyst visible — that's a positioning move, not a fundamentals move. Drone/loitering-munition names get bid hard on Middle East escalation, but historically these spikes retrace 30-50% within two weeks once the headline news fades, absent an actual contract award. The five-day magnitude is now disproportionate to what's been confirmed.
entries.what_kills: An actual large Switchblade or JUMP-20 contract announcement, or material Iran escalation (strikes, Strait closure) — either would justify the move and convert it to UNDERDONE
entries.reasoning: AeroVironment flagged OVERDONE confidence 3 on +18% one-day (+30.83% five-day) geopolitical sympathy rally with no 8-K or contract award. The thesis is narratively plausible—defense spikes on Iran headlines often fade 30-50% once the news normalizes—but confidence is only 3, and your track record on confidence-3 OVERDONE calls is weak (50% hit rate, n=14). The move lacks a company-specific catalyst (no 8-K, no contract), so it is entirely sentiment-driven. More importantly, Iran escalation risk is not a short-term fading setup if military tensions actually worsen; the flag's own 'what_kills' section acknowledges that continued escalation or a real contract would justify the move. Without a clear, near-term catalyst to fade against (like a ceasefire or explicit 'no new contracts'), taking a directional short on a confidence-3 defense sympathy play into headline uncertainty is not justified. If Iran calms visibly, the fade will be obvious and easier to short with better timing. WATCH for headlines; skip now.

entries.name: Dick's Sporting Goods Inc
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings miss with Foot Locker integration costs hitting profitability
entries.thesis: 8-K Item 2.02 + 8.01 confirms earnings event; CNBC explicitly attributes the miss to Foot Locker turnaround costs eating profitability even as the top line surges. The drop reflects a real, structural margin concern from a known M&A overhang rather than a one-time blip. Magnitude looks appropriate given DKS was a quality compounder with premium multiple — when integration risk on a contentious deal actually shows up in numbers, multiple compression of this scale is normal.
entries.what_kills: Management call clarifies Foot Locker costs are front-loaded with clear exit ramp; legacy DKS comps remain strong and the miss was solely Foot Locker accounting — then fade-the-drop becomes viable
entries.reasoning: Dick's Sporting Goods flagged RATIONAL confidence 4 on -5.97% one-day move following earnings miss tied to Foot Locker acquisition integration costs. The flag argues the drop is 'appropriate' and 'not OVERDONE,' so it is not recommending a fade or a catch—it's saying the market priced the integration drag correctly. Since the flag does not classify DKS as OVERDONE or UNDERDONE (it's RATIONAL, confidence 4), it does NOT meet the 'OVERDONE/UNDERDONE with confidence ≥ 3' filter for buy-eligible discoveries. The catalyst URL is live (8-K 2.02), but the thesis is 'this move is rationally priced,' not 'this is a buying opportunity.' SKIP is correct because the flag itself is not recommending a trade—it's describing a correct repricing.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; momentum extension against deteriorating mortgage backdrop
entries.thesis: BLDR has run sharply over the past week and added another leg today, but today's news flow includes mortgage refi demand dropping 18% as rates hit a nine-month high. That is a direct demand headwind for residential building products. The continuation move appears to be momentum-driven rather than fundamentally supported by the rate backdrop. Setup favors mean reversion if rates stay elevated.
entries.what_kills: Tape-level rate reversal lower; specific M&A or capital-return announcement from BLDR; homebuilder peers (DHI, LEN) breaking out alongside
entries.reasoning: Builders FirstSource flagged OVERDONE confidence 3 on +4.11% one-day, +16.69% five-day momentum extension into deteriorating mortgage backdrop (rates hitting 9-month high, refi demand down 18%). Thesis is that the rally is momentum-driven rather than fundamentally justified, and a mean-reversion setup favors a fade over the next 2-3 weeks. However, BLDR is already represented in the portfolio with two open positions (17 shares total, +7.32% weighted average, still within profitability). Your existing BLDR positions are on 'housing affordability bill' and director-change catalysts, which are now weakening as macro headwind asserts itself. Adding a third BLDR position on an OVERDONE fade thesis would expose you to:  (1) concentration risk (Industrials sector already ~39% of bankroll via BLDR + DCO); (2) conflicting thesis (you own BLDR long-dated on a positive bill thesis, now short-dated on a macro fade thesis—that's a hedge, not a conviction). Portfolio rules require sector diversity; adding more BLDR violates the spirit of the guardrails and creates internal contradiction. SKIP and allow the two existing positions to play out.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: 5/21 8-K with material agreement + new debt + unregistered equity issuance; today's price action is semi-sector tailwind.
entries.thesis: Today's move is mostly sector-driven (SMH +4.5%), but the 5/21 8-K bundling 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (financial obligation) + 3.02 (unregistered equity sales) + 8.01 looks like a financing/acquisition package. That combination most often signals an M&A deal funded with debt + stock issuance. Volume is actually below average today, so the catalyst is not driving incremental flow — the SMH tailwind is doing the work.
entries.what_kills: If the 8-K turns out to be a routine convertible note refinancing with no strategic element, the bundle is non-event and ONTO is purely a sector trade.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The M&A thesis relies on interpreting a specific 8-K item bundle as a 'high-probability signature,' but volume_multiple of 0.89 and the explicit statement that 'the market has NOT yet priced the deal' are contradictory—if the deal is real and unknown, why is volume below average and sector-driven momentum doing the work? Bear case: If this were a genuine material acquisition announcement (items 1.01 + 2.03 + 3.02), institutional buyers would be rushing in and volume would spike well above average. Instead, volume_multiple is 0.89 (below average) and the Haiku reasoning itself states 'the market has NOT yet priced the deal' and 'the SMH tailwind is doing the work.' This is a direct contradiction: either (a) the market doesn't know about the deal yet, in which case the 8-K bundle should have generated immediate insider/accumulator flow and spike volume, or (b) the market saw the 8-K and decided it was routine refinancing (not M&A), which is why volume stayed flat and only sector momentum moved the stock. The fact that confidence is only 3 and volume is suppressed suggests the 'M&A signature' reading may be over-interpreted from an SEC filing template structure. A routine convertible debt refinance with shelf-registered equity issuance hits items 2.03 and 3.02 without any deal. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: Onto Innovation flagged RATIONAL confidence 3 on +4.55% one-day, +7.72% five-day move tied to 5/21 8-K bundling 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (financial obligation) + 3.02 (unregistered equity sales) + 8.01. The flag's thesis is that this 8-K combination is a 'high-probability signature of M&A-with-financing' and 'worth a dedicated read.' Today's price action is mostly sector-driven (SMH +4.5%, volume is below average), so the market has NOT yet priced the deal. Confidence 3 is marginal, but the catalyst URL is live and the 8-K structure (bundle of 1.01 + 2.03 + 3.02) is a named, specific signature that can be verified quickly. This is a textbook exploratory candidate: a small position (6% / ~600 USD) to test whether the M&A thesis holds up in the press release / proxy filing. If the deal is real and reasonably priced, the stock has room to move as the market catches up. If it's a routine refinancing, ONTO is a pure SMH beta play and not worth holding. Exploratory tier lets you validate the thesis with a small amount of capital risked.)

red_team.weakest_link: The M&A thesis relies on interpreting a specific 8-K item bundle as a 'high-probability signature,' but volume_multiple of 0.89 and the explicit statement that 'the market has NOT yet priced the deal' are contradictory—if the deal is real and unknown, why is volume below average and sector-driven momentum doing the work?
red_team.critique: If this were a genuine material acquisition announcement (items 1.01 + 2.03 + 3.02), institutional buyers would be rushing in and volume would spike well above average. Instead, volume_multiple is 0.89 (below average) and the Haiku reasoning itself states 'the market has NOT yet priced the deal' and 'the SMH tailwind is doing the work.' This is a direct contradiction: either (a) the market doesn't know about the deal yet, in which case the 8-K bundle should have generated immediate insider/accumulator flow and spike volume, or (b) the market saw the 8-K and decided it was routine refinancing (not M&A), which is why volume stayed flat and only sector momentum moved the stock. The fact that confidence is only 3 and volume is suppressed suggests the 'M&A signature' reading may be over-interpreted from an SEC filing template structure. A routine convertible debt refinance with shelf-registered equity issuance hits items 2.03 and 3.02 without any deal.

Could be tied to housing/HVAC demand concerns or a sell-side note we can't see. Without a visible catalyst, can't articulate either bull (oversold/buy-the-dip) or bear (fundamental break) at conf 3+.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions

entries.thesis: Up ~14% on 2.5x volume, five-day +17% — strong but less extended than AVAV. Kratos has genuine ongoing CCA/drone program newsflow and the Iran tape provides a coherent reason. Magnitude is sharp but not yet at the extension level where a fade is high-conviction. Watch for whether this is following AVAV's setup or has its own contract catalyst.
entries.what_kills: If five-day pushes past 25%+ without contract news, fade setup becomes much more attractive

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No identified company catalyst; possibly oil-down/airline-up macro beta

Could be fuel cost tailwind from oil's collapse (Iran de-escalation), broader airline re-rating, or unreported analyst action. Without evidence I can't pick a direction at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: Peer airlines flat or down while ALK rips alone strongly suggests unknown idiosyncratic news; if oil rebounds and ALK holds, the fuel narrative breaks

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.

entries.what_kills: Move is pure short-squeeze with no fundamental news — would fade on first market-wide risk-off day

Without the actual print details (guidance, HOKA/UGG splits) I cannot tell if the continuation is justified follow-through on a beat or short-covering overshoot in a quality compounder that had been beaten down. Both bull and bear cases require the segment data I don't have.
entries.what_kills: Either side requires the actual report contents to commit.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: Possibly China steel demand read-through; no filings
entries.thesis: AMR jumped on 1.5x volume with no 8-K, no earnings, in a tape where energy is selling off hard. Met coal is decoupled from thermal/crude, so the energy divergence isn't dispositive. China industrial profits +24.7% in April could be the catalyst (steel demand proxy → met coal), but the connection is speculative without confirming flow data. Neither bull nor bear case is articulable at conf 3+ without more.
entries.what_kills: If peers don't move and AMR is alone, it's likely a low-float technical squeeze without fundamental support.

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No 8-K, no earnings within the window. Possibly sympathy with semi rotation or activist/M&A chatter (Starboard has historically held QRVO).
entries.thesis: Move has now compounded to a significant multi-day run without an 8-K or earnings event. Could be activist/M&A speculation (QRVO has been a recurring activist target), sympathy with broader semi rotation away from NVDA into smaller chip names (SMH strong, NVDA weak), or leaked sell-side note. Without a visible driver, direction-from-here is genuinely uncertain.
entries.what_kills: Discovery that the move is purely retail/momentum with no fundamental driver — would argue for fade. Alternatively, confirmed activist 13D would invalidate the 'no catalyst' framing entirely.

entries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.
entries.catalyst: May 21 8-K with 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure (content unknown from filing codes alone).
entries.thesis: BMI filed a 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure on May 21 and the stock has continued higher into today. Reg-FD filings can be either bullish (investor day reaffirming/raising outlook) or neutral (boilerplate conference deck). Without knowing the content of the filing, I cannot articulate a direction at conf 3+. BMI is a low-beta water-meter compounder that rarely moves this much without substance, which slightly biases bullish, but not enough to commit.
entries.what_kills: The 7.01 is routine investor conference boilerplate — in which case the move is sentiment/sympathy with the utility/water infrastructure theme and could fade.

entries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown. Possibly networking sympathy or short squeeze.
entries.thesis: Volume multiple is below 1x (0.89), which is unusual for a sharp single-day move and weakens the conviction that institutional buyers are accumulating on news. Could be sympathy with networking peers (ANET, CSCO) on AI-networking theme but neither moved sharply today. No 8-K. The combination of sharp move + below-average volume + no visible catalyst is a yellow flag — could be a thin-tape squeeze rather than informed buying.
entries.what_kills: Volume stays light and the move fades over the next 1-2 sessions — classic thin-tape pop pattern.
